_doc: Quarantine manifest for known-flaky tests. See scripts/ci/check-quarantine.mjs for the validation rules. Each entry MUST have an issue link and an `expires` date within 30 days of `added`. Manifest is empty by default; never add an entry without first opening a tracking issue.

vitest.reason: Intermittent cold-start/network jitter against the deployed support-chat function under parallel security-suite load. Tests pass locally and a live curl confirms the function correctly returns 403 for this origin; the origin gate runs before rate limiting, so the only plausible cause is transient edge infra latency exceeding the 30s per-test timeout.

vitest.pattern: edge-function-cors-cacheability\.test\.ts > Forbidden origins .* > support-chat > POST from "http://localhost\.evil\.com" returns a non-cacheable response with Vary: Origin
vitest.reason: Same family as the cors-custom-headers support-chat flake: intermittent cold-start/network jitter on the deployed support-chat function under parallel security-suite load. The 403 forbidden-origin path emits the full SECURITY_HEADERS bag (Cache-Control: no-store, Vary: Origin, Pragma: no-cache), verified locally and by sibling origins in the same matrix passing in the same run. Only the http://localhost.evil.com case occasionally times out or returns a transport-level failure before headers can be asserted.

vitest.pattern: code-redemption-rate-limit\.test\.ts > redeem-access-code: brute-force & replay resilience > burst of 30 parallel calls across many distinct fake codes: zero leaks, zero 5xx, stable codes
vitest.reason: 30-way parallel burst against the deployed redeem-access-code edge function occasionally surfaces a transient 5xx or non-JSON response from edge infrastructure under cold-start contention, despite the in-file warmupPromise. Function correctness verified by sibling tests in the same file (20-parallel, serial replay, OPTIONS preflight burst) which all pass; only the highest-fanout case is sensitive to edge latency tails. Handler returns deterministic 4xx with KNOWN_ERROR_CODES for every accepted request.
